prerequisites: A Nexio account with access to the Nexio Dashboard.
An API user under Settings > User Management whose username and API key you can copy (supplies the username and api_key fields).
The subdomain for your Nexio environment (for example the sandbox vs production host).
A start_date marking the earliest point from which to sync data.

authMethods.name: Basic authentication (username + API key)
authMethods.summary: Nexio authenticates API requests with HTTP Basic auth. Your Nexio username and API key are Base64-encoded and sent in an `Authorization: Basic <encoded>` header. Obtain both values from the Nexio Dashboard under Settings > User Management by selecting your API user account and copying the username and API key. These map to the connector's `username` and `api_key` (secret) fields.

setupSteps.title: Locate your API user credentials
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Nexio Dashboard and go to Settings > User Management. Select your API user account, then copy its username and API key. Nexio uses these for HTTP Basic authentication.

setupSteps.title: Provide the username and API key
setupSteps.body: Enter the copied username into the `username` field and the API key into the `api_key` (secret) field. Nexio Base64-encodes these into the `Authorization: Basic` header on each request.

setupSteps.title: Set the subdomain for your environment
setupSteps.body: Enter the `subdomain` for the Nexio environment you are connecting to. Nexio exposes separate sandbox and production hosts, so make sure the subdomain matches the environment that issued your credentials.

setupSteps.title: Set the sync start date
setupSteps.body: Provide a `start_date` to define the earliest date from which the connector should begin syncing records.
